title: How The Gogo (GOGO) Story Is Shifting With Softer 2026 Outlook And Capital Return Hopes
content:
Get insights on thousands of stocks from the global community of over 7 million individual investors at Simply Wall St.

The headline change for Gogo is a cut in the price target from US$11 to US$7, even as a separate model fair value figure remains at US$9.33 per share. That shift reflects analyst concerns around softer 2026 guidance and pressure on legacy revenue, balanced against improving leverage and the potential for capital returns. As you read on, you will see how these moving parts shape the current analyst narrative and what to watch as it continues to evolve.

Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full story. Head over to our Company Report to find new ways to value Gogo.

What Wall Street Has Been Saying

🐂 Bullish Takeaways

JPMorgan highlights that Gogo delivered better Q4 results, which supports the view that the current business model can still meet or exceed near term expectations despite pressure on older revenue streams. The same JPMorgan report points to declining leverage as a potential positive, as it could give Gogo more room to return capital to shareholders and repurchase debt in the open market.

🐻 Bearish Takeaways

JPMorgan reduced its price target on Gogo to US$7 from US$11 and maintains a Neutral rating, signaling caution around how the updated outlook lines up with the current share price. The bank cites softer 2026 guidance and legacy revenue pressure as key watchpoints, suggesting that execution on newer revenue drivers will matter more for the equity story and valuation debate.

Do your thoughts align with the Bull or Bear Analysts? Perhaps you think there's more to the story. Head to the Simply Wall St Community to discover more perspectives!NasdaqGS:GOGO 1-Year Stock Price Chart

We've flagged 3 risks for Gogo. See which could impact your investment.

What's in the News

Gogo reaffirmed its 2026 earnings guidance, projecting total revenue between US$905 million and US$945 million, with about 80% expected from service revenue and 20% from equipment revenue. The company reported no share repurchases from October 1, 2025 to December 31, 2025 under its existing buyback, having already repurchased 4,486,450 shares, or 3.51%, for US$38 million under the program launched on September 5, 2023. SD Government, a Gogo division, received US Air Force Air Mobility Command T-1 certification for its Roll-on/Roll-off Beyond Line of Sight Tactical Removeable Airborne Satellite Communications system for C-130 aircraft. This enables plug and play broadband capability across multiple variants and potential U.S. and international defense users.

Story Continues

How This Changes the Fair Value For Gogo

Fair value in the model is unchanged at US$9.33 per share. Revenue growth assumption remains about 1.74% in both the prior and updated model. Net profit margin assumption is largely stable at 11.31% in the updated inputs. Future P/E multiple remains 15.0x in both the prior and updated model. Discount rate moves slightly from 6.99% to 7.05%.

Never Miss an Update: Follow The Narrative

Narratives link a company story to a financial forecast and fair value so you can see how business developments connect to the numbers. They update automatically when new data, guidance, or analyst views come through.

Head over to the Simply Wall St Community and follow the Narrative on Gogo to stay up to date on:

How Gogo aims to tap into roughly 12,000 business jets and turboprops that do not yet have broadband, particularly outside North America. What the Galileo HDX and FDX terminals and the Satcom Direct integration could mean for new service revenue, margins, and MilGov opportunities. Key execution and technology risks, including certification timelines, 5G rollout, capital intensity, and competition from providers such as Starlink.
